title: Alignment with moby swagger definition for ContainerState.Status
body:
### Issue Description

According to moby definition, the permitted values for Status don't include the value `"stopped"`,

https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/6f14c8ee5adc314a900808b0c991e12d9d28be67/api/swagger.yaml#L4624-L4630

but that's what I got in response from an inspect container request, and my app crashed on that

```
thread 'testcontainers::test_h2spec::test_h2spec' panicked at 'called `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value: 
JsonDataError { message: "unknown variant `stopped`,
 expected one of ``, `created`, `running`, `paused`, `restarting`, `removing`, `exited`, `dead` at line 1 column 192", column: 192 }', tests/testcontainers/test_h2spec.rs:359:119
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```rust
let final_inspect = docker.inspect_container(&*container.id, Some(InspectContainerOptions { size: false })).await.unwrap();
```

### Describe the results you received

`"stopped"`

### Describe the results you expected

one of ``, `created`, `running`, `paused`, `restarting`, `removing`, `exited`, `dead`

### podman info output

comments.body:
The states on podman side seem to be specified in

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/80be8c3d6466f6094135e2712f1feea890cc7738/libpod/define/containerstate.go#L39-L70

so, comparing that to the moby states from https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/6f14c8ee5adc314a900808b0c991e12d9d28be67/api/swagger.yaml#L4624-L4630

```
  ContainerState:
    description: |
      ContainerState stores container's running state. It's part of ContainerJSONBase
      and will be returned by the "inspect" command.
    type: "object"
    x-nullable: true
    properties:
      Status:
        description: |
          String representation of the container state. Can be one of "created",
          "running", "paused", "restarting", "removing", "exited", or "dead".
        type: "string"
        enum: ["created", "running", "paused", "restarting", "removing", "exited", "dead"]
        example: "running"
```

I see that podman has several extra states: `initialized`, `stopped`, `stopping` ,and `unknown` (assuming that can ever be produced).

I need to look to the compat api in podman, as explained in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/14641#discussioncomment-2971984

So, when looking there, I see

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/80be8c3d6466f6094135e2712f1feea890cc7738/pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers.go#L293-L326

which says to me that if I were truly using the compat API, I should never see `stopped`, because it is filtered out in

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/80be8c3d6466f6094135e2712f1feea890cc7738/pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers.go#L301-L310

I thought that when I do

```
systemctl --user enable --now podman.socket
export DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/${UID}/podman/podman.sock
```

and then use app that expects docker, it uses the compat podman api. Or am I wrong about this?

You should, indeed, be using the compat API if you're pointing an app written against the Docker API at our API socket.

I think that the API status filtering may only be happening for the `/containers/json` endpoint (podman/docker ps) instead of the `/containers/$id/json` endpoint (podman/docker inspect), which might explain why you're still seeing `stopped`.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
